title: Macbook restart after shutdown when Podman was used
body:
### Issue Description

My Macbook restarts after 3 or 4 minutes when it was shutdown and a Podman machine was running just before shutting down the Mac. The same issue was reported in [Q&A](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/25307) but never answered.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Start up the Macbook
2. Open a terminal window
3. Start Podman machine with the command "podman machine start"
4. Wait until the machine is started succesfully
5. Stop Podman machine with the command "podman machine stop"
6. Shutdown the Macbook (Apple icon on the leftcorner of the screen -> Shut Down...) 

### Describe the results you received

It looks like the Mac has shutdown but after 3 or 4 minutes it is restarting
When I log in, there is a message "your computer was restarted because of a problem", when I click details, this is the comment:

---

panic(cpu 2 caller 0xfffffe00355812c8): watchdog timeout: no checkins from watchdogd in 130 seconds (41 totalcheckins since monitoring last enabled), shutdown in progress, vfs shutdown in progress, last vfs completion time: 124 seconds ago
Debugger message: panic
Memory ID: 0xff
OS release type: User
OS version: 25E253
Kernel version: Darwin Kernel Version 25.4.0: Thu Mar 19 19:31:17 PDT 2026; root:xnu-12377.101.15~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6020

---

### Describe the results you expected

When I shut down the Mac, I don't want it to reboot automaticity due to an issue with Podman.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body: before you shut down, can you use `ps` and `top` to poke around and see if any machine related processes are still running?

Hi Baude, I can use ps and top but I don't see any machine processes still running. I have adding the output of ps and top, see the table for the filenames and the description. Perhaps you spot something weird.

| command | filename | description |
| ------------- | ------------- | ------------- |
| ps | [processes-direct-after-mac-boot.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27612302/processes-direct-after-mac-boot.txt) | output of ps just after booting and log in to the macbook |
| ps | [processes-direct-after-machine-start-command.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27612301/processes-direct-after-machine-start-command.txt) | output of ps just after starting the podman machine from a command line |
| ps | [processes-direct-after-machine-stop-command.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27612300/processes-direct-after-machine-stop-command.txt) | output of ps just after stopping the podman machine from a command line |
| top | [top-direct-after-mac-boot.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27612324/top-direct-after-mac-boot.txt) | output of top just after booting and log in to the macbook |
| top | [top-direct-machine-start-command.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27612322/top-direct-machine-start-command.txt) | output of top just after starting the podman machine from a command line |
| top | [top-direct-machine-stop-command.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27612323/top-direct-machine-stop-command.txt) | output of top just after stopping the podman machine from a command line |

I am not sure about this. I was not able to reproduce this on my Mac. Also found a similar error on https://discussions.apple.com/thread/256197784?sortBy=rank 

Maybe this cloud can solve this issue: https://github.com/crc-org/vfkit/pull/316
I think Podman is still using vfkit v0.6.1. Let's update vfkit to v0.6.2 for podman 6. WDYT? @baude

Thanks @Honny1 for looking into this issue. It's hard to fix an issue when you cannot reproduce it yourself. The update of vfkit sounds promising.

Since this morning, Tahoe 26.5 is available. I'm going to install this later today but I don't think this will resolve the issue.

> Maybe this cloud can solve this issue: [crc-org/vfkit#316](https://github.com/crc-org/vfkit/pull/316)
> I think Podman is still using vfkit v0.6.1. Let's update vfkit to v0.6.2 for podman 6. WDYT? [@baude](https://github.com/baude)

I was thinking 0.8.2 ? we also need to to do this for podman-5.x as well (and right quick) bc it fixes a bad regression in vfkit.
As expected, updating to Tahoe 26.5 didn't resolve this issue.

Is it possible that you have a device connected, such as a hard drive or something similar? A related question: if you don't boot the podman machine, does the issue still occur? And what happens if you don't shut down the podman machine?

Nothing is connected to the Mac when this happens. When I have a docking station connected with a monitor, mouse and keyboard, it also happens.

The issue (message of restarted because of a problem) doesn't occur if the podman machine is not started/running.

And the issue also occur if the podman machine isn't shutdown by me before shutting down the Mac.
Okay, the fastest way to mitigate this is to try using `libkrun` as the provider if you don't need Rosetta.

The machine was already configured not to use Rosetta, see the output of "podman machine inspect". But it was using "apple hypervisor". So I removed the machine "podman-machine-default" and created a new machine from a terminal with this command:

`CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=libkrun podman machine init --cpus 8 --disk-size 102400 --memory 15360 --rootful machine-libkrun`

**Output of 'podman machine list'**
| NAME | VM TYPE | CREATED | LAST UP | CPUS | MEMORY | DISK SIZE |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| machine-libkrun | libkrun | About an hour ago | 9 minutes ago | 8 | 15GiB | 100TiB |

The disksize in TiB is strange, I don't have 100 TiB in the Mac.

But the issue is still there. After starting and stopping the machine and shut down the Mac, after 2 to 3 minutes the Macbook starts again. The detail message is still mentioning 'vfs shutdown in progress'.
Well, 100TiB seems strange, and I suspect that's the issue.
Hmm, indeed the documentation says disk size is in GB, I thought in MBs. I will correct it and hopes this will solve the issue.

I removed the machine and created a new one with this command:
`CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=libkrun podman machine init --cpus 8 --disk-size 100 --memory 15360 --rootful machine-libkrun`
and the Macbook shuts down and does not restart with a message.

I also tried this command (after modifying ~/.config/containers/containers.conf by removing libkrun):
`podman machine init --cpus 8 --disk-size 100 --memory 15360 --rootful`
and this also works.

Thanks for the help.
